title: Sexually Transmitted Infection Screening * LGBTQ2+ Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Sexually Transmitted Infection Screening * LGBTQ2+ Issues 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - LGBTQ2+ Issues Transgender Individuals Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT PRISM HEALTH PIVOT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides sexual health services that include HIV and STI testing. 2236 SE Belmont Street Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as lesbian, gay, bisexual, or trans Hours: No standard hours Email (971) 279-7033 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRUE COLORS RECOVERY RECOVERY MENTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides substance use recovery peer services led by professionals with lived experience, who help participants create a personalized recovery pathway. The program also offers a community center where individuals can access mutual aid groups, on-site STI and Hepatitis C testing, skill-building opportunities, and resource fairs. 3807 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who identify LGBTQ2+ and have a substance use disorder Hours: 7 days per week 2pm-10pm Email (971) 252-1943 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT PIVOT TRUE COLORS RECOVERY RECOVERY MENTORING
